Notice Of Motion For Protective Order To Quash Business Records Subpoenas
Off Calendar Dec-10-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Jenna Musselman Yott, Atty. for Plaintiff and moving party: William C. Yarborough
No appearances made by Defendant.

RULING: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff's Motion For Protective Order To Quash Business Records Subpoenas is granted in part and denied in part.

The motion is granted as to the subpoena to San Francisco City Clinic.

The motion is granted in part as to the subpoenas to the San Francisco Human Services Agency and the San Francisco Department Of Public Health. Items 1, 4, and 8 are ordered stricken from Attachment 3 of each of those subpoenas as they are overly broad.

The motion is granted as to the subpoena to Dr. Seymour Cohen as it invades Plaintiff's right to privacy without good cause.

Defendant's request for sanctions is denied.
Order was signed in open court.
COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Otion For Protective Order To Quash Business Records Subpoenas
